text: Could you speak more slowly, please?
This quarter is better than the last one.
Is there any news from the client?
Good morning. I have a reservation for Monday, and I want to confirm the details.
Of course, Mr. Gasparelli. How many nights are you staying with us?
Three nights. Could you speak more slowly, please? Is there any breakfast included?
Yes, breakfast is free for all guests. I will also send the confirmation to your email as an attachment.
Thank you. And how much is the room per night?
It is 180 dollars per night. Have a safe flight, Mr. Gasparelli!

Good morning, Mr. Gasparelli, this is a voicemail from the front desk team. We are confirming the details for your business trip next week. Your flight departs on Monday at nine in the morning, so please arrive at the airport two hours early for check-in and customs. Your hotel reservation is ready, and breakfast is included for all guests. On Tuesday, you have a video call with the New York office at two in the afternoon. The agenda and the figures for the quarter are attached to your email. If you need to call us back, please dial the reception. Thank you, and have a safe trip.

Five months ago, I could not handle a business trip in English. I remember the first email I had to write to a client. It took me one hour, and the subject line was wrong. On the phone, I could not understand fast speakers, so I always asked them to repeat. At the airport, I was afraid of customs, and at the hotel I pointed at the menu instead of ordering. But look at me now. I write clear emails. I make calls and ask people to speak slowly. I read the figures in a report and explain the growth and the profit. I travel, I check in, and I order dinner with confidence. The same words, used again and again, became my new professional voice.

First, Rafael writes an email to confirm the meeting and attaches the agenda. Then he makes a video call and asks the client to speak more slowly. Next, he checks the figures in the quarterly report before the trip. After that, he arrives at the airport, goes through customs, and finds his gate. Finally, he checks in at the hotel and orders dinner at the restaurant.
Could you speak more slowly, please?
Our profit is higher than last year.
Is there any news from New York?
How much is the room per night?
Could I have the bill, please?
Please find the agenda in the attachment.
I have a reservation under Gasparelli.
